item.title: Nirma Previous Year Papers
item.description: The Project use Datatables for smart searching. Smart searching performs partial and out of order word matching that is performed globally across all searchable columns. This allows for queries like “machine learning 2019” to bring up the specific paper. You can read more about smart search here . The Data present here is sourced from the official Nirma Library Archive . Examples “machine learning may 2018” “cs302” “database manage d to d” Inspiration This project is inspired by the MFQP project.

item.title: Using Computer Vision to OCR Registration Forms
item.description: Given a registration form such as this one , normal OCR techniques do not tend to work, since libraries like Tesseract and EasyOCR cannot decipher checkboxes and other box fields for written texts. Since these forms have boxes where letters need to be written, a better approach would be to first try to detect checkboxes and other boxed fields and then convert that handwritten text based on its location. Procedure using Standard OCR I printed a page of a sample registration form, wrote fake details and scanned it.

item.title: How to Scrape a Website Using Puppeteer in Node.js
item.description: Puppeteer is a Node.js library which provides a high-level API to control headless Chrome or Chromium over the DevTools Protocol. It allows automating, testing and scraping of web pages over a headless/headful browser. Installing Puppeteer: To use puppeteer, you must have Node.js installed. Then, Puppeteer can be installed in the command line using the npm package manager. npm install puppeteer Using Puppeteer: The Puppeteer library can be imported in your script using:

item.title: Timetable Application
item.description: The application creates a personalised Timetable for College based on your Core subjects, Electives and Subdivision specific Labs. This Timetable can be resized and edited. It can then be downloaded. The Timetable uses html2canvas to take a screenshot of the Timetable. It uses Javascript and JQuery to add the editing options. The application can be viewed here .

item.title: Web Scraping using lxml and XPath in Python
item.description: In this article, we will discuss the lxml python library to scrape data from a webpage, which is built on top of the libxml2 XML parsing library written in C. When compared to other python web scraping libraries like BeautifulSoup and Selenium, the lxml package gives an advantage in terms of performance. Reading and writing large XML files takes an indiscernible amount of time, making data processing easier & much faster.

item.title: About Me
item.description: Hi there 👋 I’m Prakhar Jain, a passionate Computer Science student who loves to explore new things. You can always find me working on some project or writing about something that interests me. With this website I intend to create something that serves both as a personal blog and a portfolio to showcase my work. I intend to write articles that give simple explanations to complex topics. If you’d like to connect, feel free to email me or check out my social media .
